title: Admiral Framework Handoff Protocol v1
description: Canonical JSON Schema for inter-agent handoffs. The text-based handoff format (Section 38) is a human-readable rendering of this schema. Hooks validate against this schema; agents see the text rendering.

via.description: Routing path. Direct handoffs require explicit authorization in routing rules.

task.description: What the receiving agent should do with this handoff

deliverable.description: The actual output — code diff, specification, audit report, etc.

acceptance_criteria.description: How the receiving agent knows they have successfully processed this handoff

context_files.description: File paths, documents, or artifacts the receiving agent needs loaded

token_budget_remaining.description: Remaining token budget for this task

constraints.description: Budget, deadline, and scope constraints specific to this handoff. Domain-specific constraints may be added via additionalProperties.

assumptions.description: Assumptions the sending agent made that the receiver should validate

open_questions.description: Unresolved issues the receiver should be aware of

metadata.description: Extension point for domain-specific fields. Interface contracts (fleet/interface-contracts.md) define metadata schemas for specific agent pairs via $ref.

session_handoff.description: Present only for session-boundary handoffs (work spanning context windows)

in_progress.description: What was started but not completed, with current state

blocked.description: What could not proceed and why

next_session_should.description: Prioritized list of what to do next

critical_context.description: Information the next session absolutely must have loaded
